idea: Promote all TMA tensor maps to L2_256B while keeping seed geometry shape-aware
justification: Nearby shape f16_1024x8192x8192 kept L2_256B and nanosleep; for low-M this is the least invasive data-grounded adaptation after the baseline seed required shape-fixup.

idea: port dynamic low-M WN128 scheduling from nearby low-M winner
justification: Low-M neighboring shapes improved after switching to the dynamic host/grid form with WN128 scheduling metadata.

idea: add producer nanosleep(100) on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Nearby low-M winners used producer nanosleep after WN128 narrowing.

idea: promote output tensor map to L2_256B on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Store-side cache promotion is orthogonal to the failed all-map L2 attempt.

idea: promote LHS and metadata tensor maps to L2_256B on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Low-M reuse suggests selective packed-A plus metadata promotion is worth isolating.

idea: tighten warpgroup wait depth to 0 on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Single-wave latency may benefit from a stricter wait policy.

idea: constrain WN128 dynamic variant with maxrregcount=128
justification: A moderate register cap tests occupancy relief for the tiny-M shape.

idea: constrain WN128 dynamic variant with maxrregcount=160
justification: A softer register cap may trim live ranges without forcing severe spills.

idea: add launch_bounds(384,1) to WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Explicit launch bounds may rebalance register allocation for one-wave execution.

idea: remove output padding on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: At M=128 the output tile is minimal, so padding may be overhead.

idea: increase output padding to 16 on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: A wider pad tests whether bank alignment improves low-M output staging.

idea: add producer nanosleep(200) on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: A stronger producer delay is the next pacing point after the 100-cycle test.

idea: add producer nanosleep(300) on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: If the single-wave consumer is still overdriven, a larger delay may help.

idea: recompile WN128 dynamic variant at -O3
justification: A compiler-only control checks whether dynamic host/grid changes expose optimization headroom.

idea: constrain WN128 dynamic variant with maxrregcount=192
justification: A loose register cap tests whether only minor trimming is needed.

idea: promote all tensor maps to L2_256B on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: After selective tests, uniform L2 hints are worth checking on the dynamic low-M kernel.

idea: combine all-map L2_256B with nanosleep(200) on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Nearby low-M wins combined cache promotion and producer pacing.

idea: increase META_TILE_COLS to 8 on WN128 dynamic variant
justification: Metadata fetch density can dominate at very small M.

idea: port choreo-generated 1p1c WN128 variant from nearby tuned shape
justification: By iter 19 the protocol requires a choreo-class exploration.

idea: tighten warpgroup wait depth to 0 on the seed kernel
justification: Check whether the baseline overlap policy is too conservative for one-wave execution.

idea: add launch_bounds(384,1) to the seed kernel
justification: The baseline may simply over-allocate registers for a single-wave launch.

idea: constrain the seed kernel with maxrregcount=128
justification: A moderate cap on the untouched seed directly tests occupancy relief.

idea: promote only the output tensor map to L2_256B on the seed kernel
justification: Isolate store-side promotion on the seed baseline.

idea: promote only LHS and metadata tensor maps to L2_256B on the seed kernel
justification: Isolate packed-A and metadata reuse without perturbing RHS/output behavior.

idea: add producer nanosleep(200) to the seed kernel
justification: If the seed is producer-heavy for a single CTA wave, direct pacing may recover overlap.

idea: constrain the seed kernel with maxrregcount=160
justification: A softer cap complements the 128-cap experiment on the seed.

idea: recompile the seed kernel at -O3
justification: A compiler-only control for the untouched baseline after multiple structural ideas.
